holdings.name: SCI Land & Assets
holdings.reasoning: SCILAL posted a ₹84 lakh net loss in Q4 FY26 (vs. a massive ₹224 cr loss a year ago, so year-on-year improvement is real), and full-year FY26 net profit was ₹28.82 cr with revenue up 27% — but operating losses widened sharply in Q4. A May 2026 MOU with NBCC to upgrade the Maritime Training Institute in Powai is a potential catalyst for asset monetisation, though timelines are unclear. With no scored calls yet, very thin revenue base, widening operating losses in the latest quarter, and the stock sitting just marginally above cost, short-term direction vs. the index is a near-coin-flip — no conviction to add or trim right now.
keyNews: Q4 FY26 net loss ₹84 lakh; operating losses widened to ₹20.31 cr despite 19% revenue growth YoY
FY26 full-year net profit ₹28.82 cr, revenue up 27.32% to ₹23.3 cr — annual recovery vs. prior year
SCI signed MOU with NBCC (May 6, 2026) to upgrade Maritime Training Institute Powai — asset monetisation watch

holdings.name: Vedanta
holdings.reasoning: The demerger of 4 entities (Aluminium, Power, Oil & Gas, Iron & Steel) has now completed and listed with mixed debut — Aluminium opened strongly but Oil & Gas hit a lower circuit, making net value impact for VEDL holders unclear day-to-day. Q4 FY26 profits surged ~92% YoY and a ₹7/share interim dividend (record date June 24) provides near-term income support, but parent Vedanta Resources' heavy debt load remains an overhang. Short-term direction is essentially a coin flip at this stage; no scored track record here warrants any confidence. This is information only, not financial advice.

items.name: HDFC Bank Ltd
items.why: Largest private-sector bank by assets; post-merger balance sheet normalisation and improving CASA ratio make it a core liquid banking proxy worth tracking
items.risk: Credit-deposit ratio remains elevated post HDFC merger; any NIM compression or slowdown in retail credit growth could weigh on earnings

items.name: Infosys Ltd
items.why: Second-largest Indian IT exporter with a strong deal-win pipeline and AI-service offerings; provides liquid, dollar-revenue exposure absent from your portfolio
items.risk: Client discretionary-spending cuts in the US/Europe and visa-cost pressures can dent revenue growth and margins

items.name: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd
items.why: Market leader in domestic formulations and a growing US specialty/branded generics business; adds quality pharma diversification beyond your existing PARAS holding
items.risk: US FDA observations at key plants and currency headwinds on dollar-denominated revenues are recurring risks

items.name: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd
items.why: Dominant passenger-vehicle franchise (40 %+ market share) benefiting from premiumisation and new SUV launches; gives auto-sector exposure missing from your portfolio
items.risk: Input-cost volatility (steel, aluminium) and a faster-than-expected shift to EVs where Maruti is a late mover could erode margins and share

items.name: NTPC Ltd
items.why: India's largest power generator is rapidly scaling renewable capacity (targeting 60 GW by 2032) while delivering steady regulated returns; complements your POWERGRID holding on the generation side
items.risk: Execution delays in renewable projects and coal-supply disruptions at thermal plants can impact both earnings and the clean-energy re-rating thesis

items.name: Hindustan Unilever Ltd
items.why: Blue-chip FMCG bellwether with pricing power, rural-recovery tailwinds, and consistent dividend yield; plugs the consumption/defensive gap in your portfolio
items.risk: Prolonged rural demand weakness or commodity-cost spikes (palm oil, crude derivatives) can compress volume growth and margins
